observation: Django-specific extractors (AppConfig, models/, api/urls.py, docs/*.md) produce 0 evidence anchors for Python-package projects. StackStorm uses setup.py + entry_points for domain registration, module-level __init__.py docstrings for domain description, and separate packages (st2common, st2api, st2actions, etc.) instead of Django apps.
failed_assumption: All Python repos use Django app conventions (apps.py AppConfig, models/ directory, api/urls.py URL namespaces) for domain registration and structural identity
missing_capability: setup.py/pyproject.toml entry_point extractor; module-level docstring extractor; README section mapper by heading; requirements.txt cross-package dependency extractor; MANIFEST.in / package_data structural boundary extractor
proposed_improvement: Add generic Python package extractors alongside Django-specific ones. Detect project structure type during intake (Django vs setuptools vs pyproject.toml) and select appropriate extractor set. Extractors: (1) setup_py_extractor — parse setup() call for name, description, entry_points, install_requires, packages; (2) module_docstring_extractor — extract __init__.py and top-level module docstrings; (3) readme_section_extractor — map README.md/RST headings to CEU domains; (4) requirements_extractor — cross-package dependency evidence
replay_impact: 15 candidates with 0 evidence anchors would produce evidence anchors for package-structured domains, unblocking reconciliation workflow for StackStorm
